problem: Ioana has three ropes whose lengths are 39 inches, 52 inches and 65 inches. She wants to cut the ropes into equal length pieces for magic tricks. No rope is to be wasted. What is the greatest number of inches possible in the length of each piece?

classified_questions.question: How would you approach dividing the ropes into equal length pieces?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What information do you need to find the greatest possible length of each piece?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can you identify all the factors of the lengths of the ropes?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Are there any common factors among all three rope lengths? If so, what are they?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can you determine which common factor is the greatest possible length of each piece?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other situations where finding common factors would be useful?
classified_questions.label: e
